experiences.intentions: Active piece is J at the top, with open space beneath and an irregular stack lower down. Immediate threats are low since the stack height is moderate and there is plenty of vertical room. Goal is to place the J efficiently to avoid creating holes and potentially support upcoming I/T/O/Z pieces. From the spawn area, rotating right to orientation 1 turns the J vertical with a foot to the right, which is a more flexible setup for sliding into columns on subsequent moves than the current flat orientation. A horizontal move now is premature without knowing final target; hard/soft drop now would commit high and waste placement potential. Best sub-goal is improve orientation first.
